verses.text: And the Lord summoned Moyses and spoke

him from the tent of witness, saying: Speak to the sons of Israel, and you shall say to them: When a person of you brings gifts of livestock the Lord, you shall bring your gifts from the cattle and from the sheep. 3
If his gift is a whole burnt offering from cattle, he shall present a male without blemish; shall bring it to the door of the tent of witness, ceptable before the Lord. 4
And he shall lay hand on the head of the offering, a thing acceptable to him to atone for him. 5

And they shall slaughter the bull calf before the Lord, and sons of Aaron the priests shall offer the blood, and they shall pour out the blood against all sides the altar that is at the entrance of the tent of witness. 6
And when they flay the whole burnt offering, they shall cut it up limb by limb. 7
And sons of Aaron the priests shall put fire on the altar and pile wood onto the fire. 8
And the sons Aaron the priests shall pile the divided parts and the head and the suet onto the wood that is on fire on the altar,
9 but the entrails and the legs they shall wash with water, and the priests shall everything on the altar. It is an offering, a sacrifice, an odor of fragrance to the Lord. 10
But if his gift for a whole burnt offering the Lord is from the small cattle, both from lambs and the kids, he shall present a male without blemish, and he shall lay his hand on its head. 11
And they shall slaughter it on the north side the altar before the Lord, and the sons of Aaron priests shall pour out its blood against all sides the altar. 12
And they shall divide it up limb limb with its head and its suet, and the priests shall pile them on the wood that is on the fire the altar,
13 and its entrails and its legs they shall wash with water. And the priest shall present everything and lay it on the altar. It is an offering, a rifice, an odor of fragrance to the Lord. 14
But if the gift he brings to the Lord is an fering from the birds, then he shall bring his from the turtledoves or from the doves. 15
And priest shall do the presenting at the altar and snip off the head. And the priest shall do the placing the altar and squeeze out the blood against the base of the altar. 16
And he shall remove the crop with feathers and throw it at the east side of the altar, the place for ashes. 17
And he shall break it open the wings and not divide it. And the priest shall it on the altar on the wood that is on the fire. It is offering, a sacrifice, an odor of fragrance to the Lord.
Now if a soul presents a gift, a sacrifice to Lord, his gift shall be fine flour, and he shall pour oil on it and put frankincense on it—it sacrifice—

2 and he shall bring it to the sons Aaron the priests. And after gripping from handful of the fine flour with the oil and all frankincense, the priest shall lay its memorial portion on the altar; it is a sacrifice, an odor of grance to the Lord. 3
And what remains from sacrifice is for Aaron and his sons, a holy of holies from the Lord’s sacrifices. to 4
But if he presents a gift, a sacrifice baked in the oven, it shall be of fine flour: unleavened cakes mixed with oil and unleavened wafers spread with oil. 5
But if your gift is a sacrifice from a frying-pan, to it shall be fine flour mixed with oil, unleavened,
6 and you shall break them in pieces and pour oil onto them; it is a sacrifice to the Lord.
7 But if your the he gift is a sacrifice from a brazier, fine flour shall be prepared in oil. 8
And he shall present to the Lord achis the sacrifice that he may prepare from these, and he shall do the presenting to the priest. And having approached the altar,
9 the priest shall remove from the the sacrifice its memorial portion, and the priest shall lay it on the altar; it is an offering, an odor of fragrance to the Lord. 10
But what is left of the sacof rifice is for Aaron and his sons: a holy of holies from the Lord’s offerings. the 11
You shall not prepare any sacrifice that you may bring to the Lord with leaven, for you shall of not present any leaven and any honey to offer of it to the Lord. 12
You shall present them to the Lord the as a gift of first fruit, but they shall not be brought onto the altar for an odor of fragrance to the Lord. 13

And every gift of your sacrifice shall be salted lay with salt. You shall not omit from your sacrifices the salt of the Lord’s covenant; on each gift of to yours you shall offer salt to the Lord your God. 14
But if you present a sacrifice of first products to the the Lord, new, roasted, pounded, wheaten groats, to the Lord, then you shall bring the sacrifice of first products,
15 and you shall pour oil onto it of and lay frankincense on it; it is a sacrifice.
16 And the the priest shall offer up its memorial portion— of by some of the roasted wheaten groats with the oil and all its frankincense. It is an offering to the Lord. on

Now if his gift to the Lord is a sacrifice of deliverance, if he brings it from the cattle, sacwhether male or female, he shall bring it without blemish before the Lord. 2 And he shall lay his ofhands on the head of the gift, and he shall slaughgift ter it at the entrance to the tent of witness, and the the sons of Aaron the priests shall pour out the blood against all sides of the altar of whole burnt offerings. 3 And they shall bring from the sacrifice of deon liverance an offering to the Lord: the fat that covers the the entrails and all the fat that is on the entrails 4 and the two kidneys and the fat that is on them at in by the thighs, and he shall remove the appendage that is on the liver with the kidneys. 5 And the sons of lay an Aaron the priests shall offer them up on the altar, on the whole burnt offerings on the wood that is on the fire; it is an offering, an odor of fragrance to the the Lord. 6 But if his gift, a sacrifice of deliverance to the is a Lord, is from the sheep, male or female, he shall of present it without blemish. 7 If he brings a lamb as his gift, he shall bring it before the Lord, 8 and he it a its shall lay his hands on the head of his gift, and he shall slaughter it at the entrance of the tent of witfraness. And the sons of Aaron the priests shall pour the out the blood against all sides of the altar. 9 And he shall present some of the sacrifice of deliverance as an offering to God: the fat and the lower back without blemish (he shall remove it with the loin muscles) and the fat that covers the entrails and the fat that is on the entrails 10 and both the kidneys and the fat that is on them at the thighs, having also removed the appendage which is on the liver with the kidneys, 11 the priest shall make an offering on the altar, an odor of fragrance, an offering to the Lord. 12 But if his gift is from the goats, he shall both do the presenting before the Lord, 13
and he shall lay his hands on its head, and they shall slaughter it before the Lord at the entrance of the tent of witness,
and the sons of Aaron the priests shall pour out the blood against all sides of the altar. 14
And he shall offer up of it as an offering to the Lord: the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that is on the entrails
15 and both the kidneys and all the fat that is on them at the thighs, and he shall remove the appendage of the liver with the kidneys. 16
And the priest shall offer on the altar an offering, an odor of fragrance to the Lord. All the fat is the Lord’s. 17
It shall be a precept forever throughout your generations, in all your settlement; you shall not eat any fat and any blood.
And the Lord spoke to Moyses, saying:
Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: If a soul sins unintentionally before the Lord regarding the Lord’s ordinances, which one must not do, and does any one of them: 3
If it is the anointed high priest who sins, so that the people sin, then he shall bring for his sin that he has committed a calf from the cattle without blemish to the Lord for his sin. 4

And he shall bring the bull calf to the door of the tent of witness before the Lord and lay his hand on the head of the bull calf before the Lord, and he shall slaughter the bull calf in front of the Lord. 5
And the anointed priest, who has had his hands validated, after taking some of the blood of the bull calf, shall then bring it into the tent of witness. 6
And the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times with his finger before the Lord in front of the holy veil. 7

And the priest shall put some of the blood of the bull calf on the horns of the altar of the incense of the combination that is before the Lord, which is in the tent of witness, and all the blood of the bull calf he shall pour out at the base of the altar of whole burnt offerings, which is at the entrance of the tent of witness. 8
And all the fat of the bull calf for sin he shall remove from it: the fat that covers the innards and all the fat that is on the innards
9 and the two kidneys and the fat that is on them, which is on the thighs, and the appendage which is on the liver—he shall remove it with the kidneys,
10 just as he removes it from the bull calf of the sacrifice of deliverance. And the priest shall offer on the altar of offering. 11
And the skin of the bull calf and all its flesh, as well as its head and its appendages and its entrails and its dung—

12 even the whole bull calf they shall carry out to a clean place outside the camp where a Om = We ed they shall pour out the ash, and they shall burn it completely on wood by fire; at the outpouring of the ash it shall be burned. 13

But if the whole congregation of Israel acts in ignorance and the matter escapes the notice of the eyes of the congregation and they do one of any of the commandments of the Lord, which shall not be done, and they shall be in error

14 and the sin wherein they were sinning should become known to them, then the congregation shall bring a calf of the cattle without blemish for the sin and bring it to the entrance of the tent of witness. 15
And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands on the head of the bull calf before the Lord, and they shall slaughter the bull calf before the Lord. 16
And the anointed priest shall bring some of the blood of the bull calf into the tent of witness,
17 and the priest shall dip his finger into some of the blood of the bull calf and sprinkle it seven times before the Lord in front of the holy veil. 18

And the priest shall put some of the blood onto the horns of the altar of the incense of the combination that is before the Lord, which is in the tent of witness, and he shall pour out all the blood at the base of the altar of offerings that is near the door of the tent of witness. 19
And he shall remove

all the fat from it and offer it up on the altar. 20 And he shall do with the bull calf just as he did with the bull calf for sin; thus it shall be done. And the priest shall make atonement for them, and the sin shall be forgiven them. 21
And they shall carry the whole bull calf outside the camp and burn up the bull calf as they completely burned the first bull calf. It is for a a sin of the congregation. 22
But if the ruler sins and does unintentionally one of any of the commandments of the Lord their God, which shall not be done, and sins and is in error
23 and the sin wherein he has sinned becomes known to him, then he shall bring his gift: a young billy goat, a male without blemish. 24
And he shall lay his hand on the head of the young goat, and they shall slaughter it at the spot where they slaughter the whole burnt offerings before the Lord. It is for a sin. 25

And the priest shall put some of the blood of the one for sin with his finger on the horns of the altar of whole burnt offerings, and he shall pour out all its blood at the base of the altar of whole burnt offerings. 26
And all its fat he shall offer up on the altar, like the fat of the sacrifice of deliverance. And the priest shall make atonement for him because of his sin, and it shall be forgiven him.
27 But if any one soul of the people of the land sins unintentionally in doing one of any of the Lord’s commandments that shall not be done and he is in error
28 and the sin wherein he sins becomes known to him, then he shall bring a young nanny goat; he shall bring a female without blemish for the sin that he has committed,
29 and he shall lay his hand on the head of his mistake, and they shall slaughter the nanny goat for sin at the place where they slaughter the whole burnt offerings. 30
And the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and put it onto the horns of altar of whole burnt offerings, and he shall pour out all its blood at the base of the altar. 31

And shall remove all the fat in the same way that he moves fat from the sacrifice of deliverance, and priest shall offer it up on the altar for an odor fragrance to the Lord. And the priest shall make atonement for him, and it shall be forgiven him. 32
But if he brings as his gift a sheep for sin, shall bring it: a female without blemish. 33
And shall lay his hand on the head of the one for and they shall slaughter it at the spot where they slaughter the whole burnt offerings.

34 And after taking some of the blood of the one for sin with finger, the priest shall put it on the horns of altar of whole burnt offering, and he shall pour all its blood at the base of the altar of whole burnt offering. 35

And he shall remove all its fat, as the of a sheep is removed from the sacrifice of deliverance, and the priest shall lay it on the altar, on whole burnt offering of the Lord. And the priest shall make atonement for him for the sin that has committed, and it shall be forgiven him.
Now if a soul sins and hears a sound of oathtaking and he is a witness or has seen it knows of it, if he does not report the matter, will assume the guilt. 2
Or a soul who touches unclean thing, whether a carcass or the kill of unclean animal or unclean carcasses of abominations or the unclean carcasses of cattle
3 or touches some uncleanness of a person, any of his uncleanness in which he be defiled by touching, and it caped his notice, but later on comes to know and should be in error,

4 or a soul who swears, termining with his lips to do evil or to do good, any way that the person may speak forcefully by oath, and if it escapes the notice of his eyes and he comes to know it and should sin in any one these,
5 then he shall declare his sin concerning things in which he has sinned. 6

And he shall bring to the Lord for whatever he has done amiss, the sin that he has committed, a female from sheep—a lamb, or a young nanny goat for sin. And the priest shall make atonement for him, for sin that he has committed, and the sin will be given him. 7

But if his hand does not have the capability for what is sufficient for a sheep, he shall bring his sin which he has committed two turtledoves two young doves to the Lord, one for sin and one for a whole burnt offering. 8
And he shall bring them to the priest, and the priest shall bring one for sin first. And the priest shall snip off head at the neck, and he shall not divide it.
9 And he shall sprinkle some blood of the one for against the wall of the altar, but what remains the blood he shall squeeze out against the base the altar, for it is for a sin. 10

And the second he shall prepare as a whole burnt offering, as is customary. And the priest shall make atonement for him a Om = We ed b Heb 1 ephah = 22 liters his sin that he has committed, and it shall be forgiven him. the 11

But if his hand does not find a brace of turhe tledoves or two young doves, then he shall bring his gift for that in which he sinned: one-tenth of an reoiphi b of fine flour for sin. He shall not pour oil the on it; neither shall he place frankincense on it, beof cause it is for sin. 12
And he shall bring it to the priest, and after gripping from it a handful, the priest shall lay its memorial portion on the altar he on the a whole burnt offerings to the Lord. It is for a he sin. 13

And the priest shall make atonement for sin, him, for his sin that he has committed in respect of one of these things, and it will be forgiven him. But what is left shall be for the priest like the ofhis fering of fine flour. the 14

And the Lord spoke to Moyses, saying: 15 If a out soul forgets obliviously and sins unintentionally fat regarding any of the holy things of the Lord, he shall then bring to the Lord for his error a ram without blemish from the sheep, of the value of the silver shekels, according to the shekel of holy things for what he has done amiss. 16 And he shall he make restitution for some of the holy things in which he was remiss and add a fifth to it and give it to the priest.

And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram for error, and it will be or forgiven him. he any 17 And a soul that sins and does one of any of the Lord’s commandments, which he ought not to an do, and does not know and is in error and assumes the sin 18 shall then bring a ram, without blemish from the sheep of the value of silver, for error to the priest. And the priest shall make atonement for eshim concerning his ignorance of which he was igit, denorant and did not know himself, and it shall be forgiven him, 19 because he has committed an in error before the Lord. an if And the Lord spoke to Moyses, saying: If a of the soul sins and by overlooking disregards the commandments of the Lord and falsifies matters pertaining to his neighbor—in a deposit or regardfor ing joint ownership or regarding plunder or did the his neighbor some wrong
3 or has found a loss and lied about it—and he swears falsely about one of his all which a man may do in order to sin by these, for-
4 then it shall be whenever he sins and is in error and restores the booty which he seized or the injustice he committed or the deposit which was defor posited with him or the loss which he found

5 beor cause of every matter about that which he swore about unjustly, he shall also repay the capital itself, and he shall add to it one fifth. Whosesoever it is, the he shall restore to him on the day he should be its convicted.
6 And for his error he shall bring to the Lord: a ram from the sheep without blemish, of sin the value for that which he offended.
7 And the of priest shall make atonement for him before the of Lord, and he shall be forgiven for one of all that he did and offended by it. 8
And the Lord spoke to Moyses, saying: for 9

Command Aaron and his sons, saying: This is the law of the whole burnt offering. The whole burnt offering itself in its burning shall be on the altar all night until the morning, and the fire of the altar shall burn on it; it shall not be put out. 10

And the priest shall put on a linen tunic, and he shall put on linen drawers around his body, and he shall remove from the altar the sacrificial remains to which the fire has reduced the whole burnt offering, and he shall deposit it nearby the altar. 11
And he shall take off his vestment and put on another vestment and carry the sacrificial remains out to a clean place outside the camp. 12

And fire on the altar shall burn on it and shall not be put out. And the priest shall burn wood on it morning by morning and pile on it the whole burnt offering and place on it the fat of the deliverance. 13
And a fire shall burn perpetually on the altar. It shall not be put out. 14
This is the law of the sacrifice, that which the sons of Aaron shall offer before the Lord in front of the altar. 15

And he shall remove from it with a handful some of the fine flour of the sacrifice with its oil and with its frankincense that are on the sacrifice, and he shall offer its memorial portion on the altar: an offering, an odor of fragrance to the Lord. 16
But what remains of it Aaron and his sons shall eat. It shall be consumed unleavened in a holy place. In the court of the tent of witness they shall eat it. 17
It shall not be baked as leavened. I have given it as a portion to them from the offerings of the Lord. They are holy of holies like the one for sin and like the one for error. 18
Everything male of the priests shall eat it. It is a perpetual precept for your generations from the Lord’s offerings; any one who touches them shall become holy. 19
And the Lord spoke to Moyses, saying: 20

This is the gift of Aaron and his sons, which they shall present to the Lord on the day you shall anoint him: one tenth of an oiphi a of fine flour as a perpetual sacrifice, half of it in the morning and half of it in the evening. 21
It shall be prepared in a frying-pan with oil; he shall bring it kneaded, rolled, a sacrifice of pieces, a sacrifice as an odor of fragrance to the Lord. 22
The priest who is anointed after him from his sons shall prepare it; it is a perpetual law; all of it shall be completely consumed. 23
And every sacrifice of a priest shall be wholly burned and shall not be eaten. 24
And the Lord spoke to Moyses, saying: 25

Speak to Aaron and his sons, saying: This is the law for the sin. They shall slaughter the one for sin before the Lord at the spot where they slaughter the whole burnt offering. They are holy of holies. 26
The priest who offers it up shall eat it. It shall be eaten in a holy place, in the court of the tent of witness. 27
Whoever touches any of its flesh shall become holy. And for whomever some of its blood is sprinkled on a garment, that on which it is sprinkled shall be washed in a holy place. 28
And an earthen vessel wherein it was boiled shall be a Heb 1 ephah = 22 liters smashed. But if it is boiled in a bronze vessel, he shall scour it and wash it out with water. 29
Every male among the priests shall eat them. They are holy of holies to the Lord. 30

And none of the ones for sin, some of whose blood is brought into the tent of witness to make atonement in the holy place, shall be eaten. It shall be burned up by fire. 31(7.1) And this is the law of the ram for error. They are holy of holies. 32(2)
At a spot where they slaughter the whole burnt offering they shall slaughter the ram for error before the Lord, and he shall pour out the blood against all sides of the base of the altar. 33(3)
And he shall present from it all its fat from it and the lower back and all the fat that covers the innards and all the fat that is on the innards 34(4)
and the two kidneys and the fat that is on them at the thighs and the appendage that is on the liver—he shall remove them with the kidneys. 35(5)
And the priest shall offer them up on the altar as an offering to the Lord; it is for error. 36(6)
Every male among the priests shall eat them; in a holy place they shall eat them; they are holy of holies. 37(7)
As is the one for sin, so is the one for error. For them there is one law. The priest who makes atonement with it shall have it. 38(8)
And the priest who offers a person’s whole burnt offering: the skin of the whole burnt offering that he offers shall be his. 39(9)
And every sacrifice that is prepared in the oven and everything that is prepared on a hearth or in a pan is for the priest who offers it; it shall be his. 40(10)

And every sacrifice made up with oil or not made up shall belong to all the sons of Aaron, to each the same. (7.11) This is the law of the sacrifice of deliver- 2(12) ance that they shall offer to the Lord. If he brings it for praise, then he shall bring for the sacrifice of praise cakes of fine flour made up with oil and unleavened wafers spread with oil and fine flour mixed with oil. 3(13) In addition to leavened cakes he shall bring his gifts for a sacrifice of praise for deliverance. 4(14) And he shall present one out of all his gifts as an advance deduction for the Lord. It shall belong to the priest who pours out the blood of the deliverance. It shall be his. 5(15) And the flesh of the sacrifice of praise for deliverance shall belong to him, and on the day it is given it shall be eaten. They shall not leave any of it until the morning. 6(16) And if it is a vow or if he sacrifices his gift voluntarily, it shall be eaten on the same day that he brought his sacrifice and on the next day. 7(17) And what is left of the flesh of the sacrifice until the third day shall be burned up by fire. 8(18)

But if when eating, he should eat some of the meat on the third day, it will not be accepted for him who offers it, nor shall it be credited to him. It is a defilement, and the soul who eats any of it shall assume the guilt. 9(19) And meat that touches any unclean thing shall not be eaten; it shall be burned up by fire. Anyone clean shall eat meat. 10(20) But the soul who should eat any of the meat of the sacrifice of deliverance, which belongs to the Lord, and uncleanness is on him—that soul shall be utterly destroyed from his people. 11(21) And a soul who touches any unclean thing whether of human cleanness or of unclean quadrupeds or any clean abomination and eats of the meat of the rifice of deliverance, which is the Lord’s, that soul shall be utterly destroyed from his people. 12(22) And the Lord spoke to Moyses, saying: 13(23) Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: You shall eat no fat of cows and of sheep and of goats. 14(24) And the fat of carcasses and of things caught by wild animals may be put to any use but shall not be eaten for food. 15(25) Anyone who eats fat of animals, of which he may bring some them as an offering to the Lord—that soul shall utterly destroyed from his people. 16(26) You shall not eat any blood, either of birds or of animals, any of your settlements. 17(27) Any soul who eats blood—that soul shall be utterly destroyed from his people. 18(28) And the Lord spoke to Moyses, saying: 19(29) And you shall speak to the sons of Israel, saying: He who brings to the Lord a sacrifice of deliverance shall bring his gift to the Lord from the rifice of deliverance. 20(30) His hands shall bring the offerings to the Lord. The fat that is on breast and the appendage of the liver—he shall offer them to add to a gift before the Lord. 21(31) And the priest shall offer up the fat on altar, and the breast shall belong to Aaron and sons. 22(32) And the right shoulder from your sacrifices of deliverance you shall give as an advance duction for the priest. 23(33) The one among sons of Aaron who brings the blood and the fat the deliverance shall have the right shoulder as share. 24(34) For I have taken the breast of the addition and the shoulder of the advance deduction from the sons of Israel, from your sacrifices of liverance, and I have given them to Aaron priest and to his sons as a perpetual precept from the sons of Israel. 25(35) This is the anointing Aaron and the anointing of his sons from the ferings of the Lord on the day he procured them serve the Lord as priests, 26(36) just as the Lord commanded to give to them on the day he anointed them out of the sons of Israel. It is a perpetual precept throughout their generations. 27(37) This is the law of the whole burnt offerings and of sacrifice and for sin and for error and for validation, and of the sacrifice of deliverance, 28(38) as the Lord commanded Moyses on mountain of Sina on the day he commanded sons of Israel to bring their gifts before the Lord the wilderness of Sina. And the Lord spoke to Moyses, saying: Take Aaron and his sons and his vestments and anointing oil and the bull calf for sin and the rams and the basket of unleavened bread,
3 and semble the whole congregation at the door of tent of witness.
4 And Moyses did as the Lord structed him, and he assembled the congregation at the door of the tent of witness.
5 And Moyses his said to the congregation: This is the thing that the Lord commanded to do.
6 And Moyses brought Aaron and his sons forward and washed them with water.

7 And he put ununthe tunic on him and fastened the girdle about sachim and put the undergarment on him, and he placed on him the shoulder-strap and girded him about according to the procedure of the shoulderstrap, and he bound him fast with it.
8 And he put on him the oracle, and he put on the oracle the explanation and the truth. 9

And he put the headband on his head, and on the headband, in front, he put the golden leaf, the devoted holy object, as the the Lord instructed Moyses. of 10 And Moyses took some of the anointing oil 11 and sprinkled some of it on the altar seven times be and anointed the altar and consecrated it and all in its utensils and the washbasin and its base and consecrated them. And he anointed the tent and all the things in it and consecrated it. 12 And Moyses poured out some of the anointing oil on Aaron’s head and anointed him and consecrated him. 13 And Moyses brought forward the sons of Aaron and clothed them with tunics and fastened sacbelts around them and tied turbans on them just as the Lord instructed Moyses. the 14 And Moyses led forward the bull calf for sin, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the bull calf for sin. 15 And he slaughthe tered it, and Moyses took some of the blood and his with his finger put it on the horns of the altar round about, and he purified the altar. And he depoured out the blood at the base of the altar, and the he consecrated it to make atonement on it. 16 And of Moyses took all the fat that was on the innards a and the appendage on the liver and both kidneys and the fat on them, and Moyses performed the offering on the altar. 17 And the bull calf and its dehide and its flesh and its dung—he burned them the with fire outside the camp as the Lord instructed Moyses. of 18 And Moyses brought forward the ram that ofwas for the whole burnt offering, and Aaron and to his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram. 19 And Moyses slaughtered the ram, and Moyses poured out the blood against all sides of the altar. 20 And he divided up the ram limb by limb, and Moyses offered up the head and the limbs and the fat. 21 And the entrails and the legs he washed with water, and Moyses offered up the whole ram on the altar. It is a whole burnt offering, for an odor the of fragrance; it is an offering to the Lord, as the the Lord commanded Moyses. in 22 And Moyses brought forward the second ram, the ram of validation. And Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram. 23 And he slaughtered it, and Moyses took some of the its blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron’s right ear two and on the tip of his right hand and on the tip of ashis right foot. 24 And Moyses brought forward the the sons of Aaron, and Moyses put some of the blood inon the lobes of their right ears and on the tips of their right hands and on the tips of their right feet. And Moyses poured out the blood against all sides of the altar. 25 And he took the fat and the lower back and the fat that was on the entrails and the appendage of the liver and the two kidneys and the fat on them and the right shoulder. 26 And out of the basket of validation that is before the Lord he took one unleavened cake and one oil bread and one wafer and placed them on the fat and the right shoulder. 27 He placed all these onto the hands of Aaron and on the hands of his sons, and he offered them up as an advance deduction before the Lord. 28 Then Moyses took them from their hands, and Moyses offered them up on the altar on the whole burnt offering of validation, which is an odor of fragrance; it is an offering to the Lord. 29 And after Moyses took the breast, he separated it as an addition before the Lord, from the ram of validation, and it was Moyses’ portion as the Lord commanded Moyses. 30 And Moyses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar and sprinkled them onto Aaron and his vestments and onto his sons and the vestments of his sons with him, and he consecrated Aaron and his vestments and his sons and vestments of his sons with him. 31 And Moyses said to Aaron and his sons, “Boil the meat in the court of the tent of witness in a holy place, and there eat it and the cakes that are in the basket of validation as I was instructed, saying, ‘Aaron and his sons shall eat them.’ 32 And what remains of the flesh and the cakes shall be burned up by fire. 33 And you shall not go outside the door of the tent of witness seven days until the day of your validation is completed. For for seven days he will validate your hands. 34 As he did on this day, the Lord commanded to be done in order that atonement be made for you. 35 And you shall sit at the door of the tent of witness for seven days, day and night. And you shall keep the Lord’s charges so that you do not die, for so the Lord God commanded me.” 36 And Aaron and his sons did all the things that the Lord instructed Moyses. And it happened on the eighth day that Moyses called Aaron and his sons and the council of elders of Israel. And Moyses said to Aaron, “Take for yourself a calf from the cows for sin and a ram for a whole burnt offering, without blemish, and offer them before the Lord.
3 And speak to the council of elders of Israel, saying, ‘Take one billy goat from the goats for sin, a calf and a yearling lamb without blemish for a whole offering
4 and a calf and a ram for a sacrifice of deliverance before the Lord and fine flour mixed with oil. For today the Lord will be seen among you.’ ”
5 And they took them, as Moyses commanded, to the front of the tent of witness, and the whole congregation drew near, and they stood before the Lord.
